title: Compose Specification, Services, Projects, Dependencies, and Lifecycle · Image and Runtime Lens: Docker Compose Dependency Readiness
summary: A web, worker, and database stack needs one portable declared model with predictable naming, dependencies, health gates, and teardown. Planned artifact: Add and verify the docker compose dependency readiness increment in validated multi-service Compose application model. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Use depends_on conditions, healthchecks, completed jobs, required dependencies, and application retry without confusing order with readiness.
Define image or build source, command, entrypoint, environment, user, working directory, health, resources, and metadata per service.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# docker-basics cumulative container delivery portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic manifest and evidence review only.
# They never connect to a Docker daemon, mount a socket, or execute learner commands.
workspace: docker
version-baseline:
  engine: 29.6.1
  compose: 5.3.1
  buildx: 0.35.0
  buildkit: 0.31.1
  dockerfile-frontend: 1.25
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-environment
evidence:

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Name client, daemon, builder, image, container, host-kernel, storage, network, registry, and supply-chain boundaries; predict state and authority; review the smallest deterministic manifest change; then verify a failure, repair, cleanup, and live-environment transfer limit. Apply that move specifically to Docker Compose Dependency Readiness while producing validated multi-service Compose application model.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A worked example three-service project has hidden interpolation, name collisions, start-order assumptions, a mutable image, and teardown that may delete intake data. Render the model, dry-run lifecycle, and prove preservation. Competency probe: Use depends_on conditions, healthchecks, completed jobs, required dependencies, and application retry without confusing order with readiness. A defensible solution applies Docker Compose Dependency Readiness, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to validated multi-service Compose application model.

content.code:
# Worked focus: docker-compose-dependency-readiness — Use depends_on conditions, healthchecks, completed jobs, required dependencies, and application retry without confusing order with readiness.
  # Evidence: docker-worked-docker-compose-model-lifecycle-3
  - competency: docker-worked-docker-compose-model-lifecycle-3
    artifact: compose.yaml
    decision: worked docker compose model lifecycle 3
    scenario-variant: 2
    environment: IPv6-enabled Compose project on a shared CI runner
    fault-injection: rebuild after the approved base digest is withdrawn
    retained-proof: accessible recovery runbook with rollback rehearsal timing
    case-key: worked3
    change: |
      services:
      api:
      image: registry.example.test/intake@sha256:verified
      depends_on:
      db:
      condition: service_healthy
      volumes:
      intake-data:
    verify: docker compose config --quiet && docker compose --dry-run up --build
    failure: Reject start order as readiness proof, implicit project identity, floating images, or teardown that silently deletes required volumes.
    repair: Render the merged model, pin identities, encode health dependencies, inspect the dry run, and document down versus down --volumes.
content.caption: Runnable Docker artifact and transfer-evidence contract example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: Short depends_on waits until every dependency is ready to serve requests. This fails the Docker Compose Dependency Readiness contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

# Evidence: docker-compose-dependency-readiness-t7eo3y
  - competency: docker-compose-dependency-readiness
    artifact: compose.yaml
    decision: compose dependency readiness
    scenario-variant: 3
    environment: multi-tenant build farm using an isolated BuildKit driver
    fault-injection: cancel during export after one platform finishes
    retained-proof: rendered Compose model and dependency health transition
    case-key: t7eo3y
    change: |
      services:
      api:
      image: registry.example.test/intake@sha256:verified
      depends_on:
      db:
      condition: service_healthy
      volumes:
      intake-data:
    verify: docker compose config --quiet && docker compose --dry-run up --build
    failure: Reject start order as readiness proof, implicit project identity, floating images, or teardown that silently deletes required volumes.
    repair: Render the merged model, pin identities, encode health dependencies, inspect the dry run, and document down versus down --volumes.
